demographics.living_situation: lives alone in a private residence

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: When I look straight at the door frame, it looks completely bent and wavy, and there is a dark smudge right in the middle of my vision. It came on over the last day or two and it's really frightening me.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My sight in one eye has suddenly got much worse over the last 48 hours and everything straight looks distorted — I already have a bit of macular trouble but this is different.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden worsening over the past 48 hours, in the right eye
symptom_narrative.duration: about two days and no better
symptom_narrative.character: straight lines like the door frame look bent and wavy, and there is a dark blank patch right in the centre of my vision
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst my vision has ever been in that eye — I can't read or make out faces when I look straight at them
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the middle of the vision in my right eye
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, does not come and go
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing seems to help; covering the bad eye lets me get around
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to read or look straight at something
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no pain in the eye, no flashing lights, no shower of floaters, and no curtain coming down over my sight; I can still see out of the sides so I don't bump into things
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I was told a couple of years ago I had the 'dry' kind of macular degeneration and my central vision has been slowly getting worse, but nothing sudden like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally independent but now very anxious, struggling with anything needing fine central vision like reading the phone, and worried about falling

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: dry age-related macular degeneration diagnosed about two years ago
past_medical_history.how_managed: monitoring only, gradual slow decline in central vision until now

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: poor adaptation to low light for some time

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone, normally independent, an adult child is contactable by phone; very anxious about going blind and about falling

communication_style.typical_message_style: clear but frightened, keeps returning to the fear of permanent blindness

objective_data_offered.value: when I cover my left eye and look at a straight edge with my right eye, the line looks bent and wavy and there's a dark spot in the centre
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-description over chat; not a validated Amsler-grid test and not clinician-confirmed

objective_data_offered.value: I can still see clearly out of the edges of that eye and can move around the room without bumping into things
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-report of peripheral vision; not formally tested
